pages.full_text:
We walked into town, stopping at __Goethe's Summer House__. It was given to him by Duke Carl Augustus. The house is very like Sho House, wooden, cosy. Goethe was a poet, politician, pressed flowers, collected minerals & stones, loved his garden. (Polymath of Soalton)
   We visited the church of St. Peter & Paul where Lucas Cranach the elder completed a triptyd altarpiece 1553, before he died. Lots of Protestant Symbolism, & portraits of Luther, Gatch & patrons. J.S. Bach played the organ 1708-17. Franz Liszt 1848 was choirmaster too & set up a School of Music.
   We had a delicious sausage lunch in the square, looked at the outside of Schiller's house & saw the Bauhaus museum. The furniture looked quite worn & dingy, but had some beautiful lines- very new at the time, 1919-1930. Eve's dressing table light.
   The boys had mega icecreams in the square. (John & Charles gave me a bit) We walked back to the house. Simon walked to the supermarket. Eve did a wash. Simon got supper ready. It's lovely in the garden
pages.notes: All major sights mentioned (Goethe's Summer House, church of St. Peter & Paul, Schiller's house, Bauhaus museum) are located in Weimar, Germany. 'Sho House' is unclear — could read 'Sho' or possibly 'Show' or another word; best reading given. 'Polymath of Soalton' is uncertain — 'Soalton' may be a misreading; could be a place name or a word like 'Sealton' or similar. 'triptyd' is the diarist's spelling of 'triptych'. 'Gatch' likely refers to 'Cranach' (repeated) or another reformer — possibly 'Gauch'; unclear. No diary date appears on this page; the Bauhaus dates mentioned (1919-1930) and historical dates (1553, 1708-17, 1848) are all historical references within the narrative, not diary entry dates. Year of diary entry cannot be determined from this page alone; the Bachhaus Eisenach ticket on the facing page (8.00 EURO) confirms post-January 2002, and volume A ends 2003, so entry is 2002 or 2003. No handwritten page number visible in top-left corner.

photo_captions: Neue Bachgesellschaft Museum — EINTRITT BACHHAUS 8,00 EURO EISENACH — 00586
pages.notes: Right page contains only a glued-in printed admission ticket for the Bachhaus (Bach House Museum) in Eisenach, Germany. Ticket number 00586. The ticket reads: 'Neue Bachgesellschaft Museum' (ornate printed design with classical figures), 'EINTRITT BACHHAUS 8,00 EURO EISENACH'. No handwritten text on this page. The 8.00 EURO price confirms this diary entry dates to post-January 2002. The text on the left page continues beyond the bottom of the page (ends 'It's lovely in the garden') and presumably continues on the next spread.
